title: Add cloche-base Docker image and simplify default Dockerfile

Make docker-build depend on docker-base. Simplify the cloche project's .cloche/Dockerfile and the init template to use cloche-base:latest. See docs/plans/2026-03-06-cloche-base-docker-design.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- docker/cloche-base/Dockerfile exists (debian:12-slim, git, cloche-agent; no node/claude-code)
- make docker-base builds cloche-base:latest and cloche-base:<version>
- make docker-build depends on docker-base
- make install builds and starts correctly
- .cloche/Dockerfile simplified to FROM cloche-base:latest + add claude-code
- cloche init generates Dockerfile using cloche-base:latest; --image flag renamed to --base-image

Replace the old gitignore strategy (ignore .cloche/*/ then negate) with the inverse: track everything, explicitly ignore runtime artifacts (.cloche/*-*-*/, .cloche/run-*/, .cloche/attempt_count/). See docs/plans/2026-03-06-cloche-init-design.md.

title: Rewrite engine as DAG walker

Display the final directory name from the project path (e.g. 'myproject' from '/home/user/workspace/myproject'). When project names conflict (e.g. foo/bar and baz/bar), show the parent directory to disambiguate (e.g. 'foo/bar' vs 'baz/bar').

title: Chore: update .gitignore to new cloche pattern
description: After cloche-07g merges, update .gitignore in the cloche project to use the new pattern: remove old .cloche/*/ negation rules, add .cloche/*-*-*/ and .cloche/run-*/ and .cloche/attempt_count/ as explicit ignores. Commit the change.

- .gitignore has .cloche/*-*-*/ (replaces old .cloche/*/)
- .gitignore has .cloche/run-*/
- .gitignore has .cloche/attempt_count/
- Old negation rules (!.cloche/prompts/ etc.) are removed
- git status shows no unintended tracked/untracked changes in .cloche/

title: Rename .cloche/config to .cloche/config.toml
description: Rename the per-project settings file from .cloche/config to .cloche/config.toml. No backwards compatibility needed. cloche init creates a default config.toml with all settings documented. See docs/plans/2026-03-06-config-toml-design.md.

title: Implement orchestration loop in cloched
description: Event-driven orchestration loop inside cloched. Triggers on: daemon startup, run completion, and new cloche orchestrate CLI command. On trigger: for each project with orchestration enabled, query task tracker, check per-project concurrency limits, claim highest-priority task, generate prompt, dispatch run. Per-project config in .cloche/config.toml. See docs/plans/2026-03-03-next-features-design.md §1.
acceptance_criteria: Orchestrator triggers on startup, run completion, and manual command. Respects per-project concurrency limits. Dispatches runs for ready tasks.

title: Update generic adapter to use result extraction
description: Replace resultOrDefault with protocol.ExtractResult. Marker overrides exit code. Exit 0 = success, non-zero = fail as fallback. Update tests. Implementation plan Task 4.

title: Add post-run log archival with SQLite indexing
description: On run completion, extract all log files from container to .cloche/<run-id>/output/. Write index entries to SQLite (run ID, step name, log file path, size, timestamps). Dashboard and CLI query index and serve file contents on demand. Enhance cloche logs <run-id> --step <name> filtering. See docs/plans/2026-03-03-next-features-design.md §4.
acceptance_criteria: Logs extracted and archived on completion. SQLite index populated. cloche logs --step filtering works.

title: Define TaskTracker port and implement Beads adapter
description: Create ports.TaskTracker interface with ListReady, Claim, Complete, Fail methods. Task struct carries ID, title, description, labels, priority. Implement first adapter for Beads. ListReady returns tasks ordered by priority. See docs/plans/2026-03-03-next-features-design.md §1.
acceptance_criteria: TaskTracker interface defined. Beads adapter works: lists ready tasks by priority, claims/completes/fails tasks.

title: Bug: Orchestrator does not close beads tasks after successful workflow runs
description:
When a workflow run completes successfully, the orchestrator never calls tracker.Complete() to mark the beads task as done. The task stays 'ready' in beads, so subsequent orchestration cycles re-dispatch it indefinitely. This caused duplicate runs and cascading merge conflicts.

Root cause: orchestrator.Run() claims tasks and dispatches runs, but OnRunComplete() only decrements the in-flight counter — it never updates the task's status in the tracker.

Fix: After a successful workflow run, call pc.Tracker.Complete(ctx, task.ID). After a failed run, call pc.Tracker.Fail(ctx, task.ID). This requires correlating run IDs back to task IDs, which is not currently tracked.
acceptance_criteria:
- Successful workflow runs mark the associated beads task as completed
- Failed workflow runs mark the associated beads task appropriately (fail or reopen)
- A task is never dispatched more than once concurrently
- Run-to-task mapping is tracked (e.g. in the Run struct or a side map)

title: Audit Logger
description: JSONL append-only log, file snapshots for revert, knowledge base updates. See Task 15 in docs/plans/2026-02-24-self-evolution-implementation.md

title: Implement per-project health calculator (indicator lights)
description: Domain-level health calculator: given last N runs for a project, return health status (green/yellow/red/grey/blue). Window size configurable per-project, default 5. Green = all passed, Yellow = some failed, Red = all failed, Grey = no runs, Blue = all in-progress. See docs/plans/2026-03-03-next-features-design.md §2.
acceptance_criteria: Health function returns correct status for all edge cases. Window size configurable.

acceptance_criteria: Given a task, produces a well-formed prompt. Uses project's configured LLM. Prompt is suitable for cloche run --prompt.

title: Add cloche delete command for container removal
description: Add 'cloche delete <container-or-run-id>' CLI command. Accepts Docker container name/ID or run ID. Calls daemon via new DeleteContainer gRPC RPC. Daemon calls docker rm and updates SQLite. Single container at a time. See docs/plans/2026-03-03-next-features-design.md §5.
acceptance_criteria: cloche delete accepts container ID or run ID. Container is removed. SQLite record updated.

title: Implement merge agent (LLM-driven merge in git worktree)
description: Merge agent runs on the host in a git worktree. Given a branch name, it: creates a worktree, pulls latest main, merges the feature branch, resolves conflicts via LLM, runs validation if configured, merges to main on success. On success: cleans up worktree and deletes the branch. On failure: marks merge as failed, preserves branch, notifies via dashboard/indicator lights. See docs/plans/2026-03-03-next-features-design.md §1 Merge Queue.
acceptance_criteria: Merge agent creates worktree, merges branch, resolves conflicts. Success: branch deleted, worktree cleaned. Failure: branch preserved, merge marked failed.

title: Add container status to dashboard run detail page
description: Run detail page shows whether the container is still available. If so, shows a 'Delete container' button. Project overview shows count of retained containers. See docs/plans/2026-03-03-next-features-design.md §5.
acceptance_criteria: Run detail shows container availability. Delete button works. Project cards show retained container count.

title: Full integration test pass
description: Run go test and go vet to verify everything works end to end. Fix any remaining issues. Implementation plan Task 12.

title: Bug: Orchestrator ignores beads dependency graph — dispatches blocked tasks
description:
The orchestrator's ListReady call returned tasks that have unsatisfied blocking dependencies. Tasks like cloche-07g (blocked by cloche-2cc, cloche-9ea, cloche-dzz) and cloche-208 (blocked by cloche-07g) were dispatched even though their blockers were still open.

Either the beads tracker's ListReady implementation doesn't check the dependency graph, or the orchestrator is calling the wrong method. This caused tasks to run before their prerequisites were complete, leading to incorrect implementations and merge conflicts.
acceptance_criteria:
- ListReady only returns tasks whose blocking dependencies are all closed/completed
- Tasks with open blockers are never dispatched by the orchestrator
- Verify with a test: create task A blocking task B, confirm B is not returned by ListReady

title: Add config file for cloched
description: Add a config file for the cloched daemon so settings like HTTP port, HTTP dashboard enabled, default image, etc. can be configured persistently instead of requiring env vars (CLOCHE_HTTP, CLOCHE_ADDR, etc.) every time the daemon starts.

title: Bug: Orchestrator has no deduplication — same task dispatched multiple times
description:
When OnRunComplete triggers a new orchestration cycle, the orchestrator can dispatch runs for tasks that already have an in-flight run (from a previous cycle or manual dispatch). There is no check to see if a task is already being worked on.

Combined with the bug where tasks aren't closed after completion, this leads to N duplicate runs of the same task, all racing to merge conflicting changes.

Fix: Track which task IDs have in-flight runs. Before dispatching, check if the task already has an active run. Skip if so.
acceptance_criteria:
- A task with an in-flight run is never dispatched again
- The in-flight tracking covers both orchestrator-dispatched and manually-dispatched runs
- After a run completes, the task becomes eligible for re-dispatch only if it was re-opened

title: Fix update-docs step failing with 'prompt too long'
description: The update-docs step fails because assemblePrompt() in internal/adapters/agents/prompt/prompt.go injects all .cloche/output/*.log files as 'Validation Output' via readFeedback(). By the time update-docs runs, implement.log (full Claude stream-json, 50KB+) is in the output dir, blowing past the context limit. The feedback injection is designed for the fix step but fires indiscriminately for all agent steps.
acceptance_criteria:
- update-docs step completes without 'prompt too long' error
- fix step still receives validation feedback
- Make feedback opt-in: add a step config key (e.g. include_feedback=true) to control whether readFeedback() output is injected into the prompt
- Only the fix step should set include_feedback=true in the default workflow template
- Default is no feedback injection when the key is absent

title: Wire web UI Trigger Orchestrator button to actual orchestrator
description:
The web dashboard's Trigger Orchestrator button (POST /api/projects/{name}/trigger) is a stub that returns 202 without calling the orchestrator. Wire it up:

1. Add an orchestrateFn field (func(ctx context.Context, projectDir string) (int, error)) to the web Handler struct.
2. Add a WithOrchestrateFunc HandlerOption to set it.
3. In handleAPITriggerOrchestrator, resolve the project name to its directory (via the run store's ListProjects), call orchestrateFn, and return the dispatched count or an error.
4. In cmd/cloched/main.go, pass web.WithOrchestrateFunc(...) when constructing the web handler, using the same function wired to the gRPC server's SetOrchestrateFunc.
5. Add a unit test in handler_test.go verifying the trigger endpoint calls the function and returns the count.
6. If orchestrateFn is nil, return 501 Not Implemented.

title: Auto-rebuild stale containers on cloche run
description: Validate that the container image is up-to-date before launching a workflow run. If the project Dockerfile or base image has changed since the last build, rebuild automatically so the container runs the correct cloche-agent and dependencies. This check should happen in the daemon when processing a RunWorkflow request.

title: Implement container retention policy for failed runs
description: Change container lifecycle so failed runs automatically keep their container for debugging. Successful runs continue to auto-delete. Preserve --keep-container flag for keeping containers regardless of outcome. Track retained containers in SQLite (container ID, run ID, project, creation time, status). See docs/plans/2026-03-03-next-features-design.md §5.
acceptance_criteria: Failed runs keep container. Successful runs delete container. --keep-container keeps regardless. Retained containers tracked in SQLite.

title: DSL parser
description: Parser that converts tokens into domain.Workflow. Handles steps, fields, wiring, container blocks. See implementation plan Task 5.
close_reason: Parser with type inference, container blocks, wiring

title: Workflow domain types
description: Workflow, Step, Result, Wire types with Validate() and NextStep(). See implementation plan Task 2.
close_reason: Workflow, Step, Wire types with Validate and NextStep

title: Bug: Merge agent resolves conflicts incorrectly — blindly picks sides
description:
Replace the merge agent's current strategy (merge + LLM conflict resolution) with a rebase-first approach that treats main as truth.

**Current behavior:** Creates a worktree at main, runs `git merge` on the feature branch, then uses an LLM to resolve conflicts. The LLM doesn't understand which side is correct, leading to reverted changes, committed runtime artifacts, and corrupted state.

**New algorithm:**
1. Create worktree at the **feature branch** (not main)
2. `git rebase -X ours main` — replays branch commits on top of current main. `-X ours` in rebase context means 'prefer main's state for conflicting hunks', preserving main's changes while applying the branch's non-conflicting work.
3. **Rebase succeeds** → `git update-ref refs/heads/main <rebased HEAD>` (fast-forward)
4. **Rebase fails** (even with `-X ours`) → `git rebase --abort`, mark merge entry as failed, log for human review. Preserve the branch for manual investigation.
5. Clean up worktree. Delete branch only on success.

**What gets removed:**
- LLM-based conflict resolution (`resolveConflicts`, `applyResolvedFiles`, `parseFileSections`)
- The `LLM` field on `MergeAgent` (no longer needed)
- The merge-based approach entirely

**What stays:**
- Serial queue processing (`ProcessQueue` recursive drain)
- Worktree-based isolation
- Validation hook (kept but not wired — future use for test-during-merge)
- `gitEnv()` helper
acceptance_criteria:
- Merge uses rebase-first strategy: git rebase -X ours main
- On clean rebase: main is fast-forwarded to rebased HEAD, branch deleted
- On rebase failure: merge flagged as failed, branch preserved, logged for human review
- LLM conflict resolution code removed from merge agent
- Serial queue processing preserved (one merge at a time)
- Existing merge agent tests updated to reflect new behavior
- ProcessQueue still drains the queue after each successful merge

title: Implement unified log stream in cloche-agent
description: Agent captures all output chronologically to .cloche/output/full.log with structured entries: timestamps, type prefixes ([status], [script], [llm]). LLM conversation output and script stdout/stderr interleaved in real-time. Per-step files (.cloche/output/llm-<step>.log, .cloche/output/<step>.log) written additionally for convenience. See docs/plans/2026-03-03-next-features-design.md §4.
acceptance_criteria: full.log contains interleaved status, script, and LLM output. Per-step files also written. All entries timestamped and typed.

title: Evolution integration test
description: End-to-end test with scripted LLM exercising full pipeline. See Task 19 in docs/plans/2026-02-24-self-evolution-implementation.md

title: Simplify code agent switching with fallback chains
description: The process of switching between code agents (Claude, Gemini, Codex, etc.) is onerous. Make it easier to configure multiple agents simultaneously and swap which one is active. Support fallback chains so that if one agent errors, the system can automatically try another (e.g. use Claude unless erroring, then fall back to Gemini or Codex).

title: Evolution Orchestrator
description: Main pipeline wiring: Collector -> Classifier -> Reflector -> branch routing -> AuditLogger. See Task 16 in docs/plans/2026-02-24-self-evolution-implementation.md

title: Chore: build cloche-base image locally (make docker-base)
description: After cloche-028 merges and is installed, run 'make docker-base' to build the cloche-base:latest image locally. Verify subsequent cloche runs use the new leaner image. Also rebuild the cloche project image with 'make docker-build'.

title: Status protocol extensions for capture
description: Add PromptText, AgentOutput, AttemptNumber fields to StatusMessage. Add StepStartedWithPrompt and StepCompletedWithCapture methods to StatusWriter. See Task 4 in docs/plans/2026-02-24-self-evolution-implementation.md
